name: makerchip
description: VS Code extension for Makerchip IDE integration

makerchip.autoUpdateResources.description: Automatically update Makerchip reference data on activation

enumDescriptions: Ask whether to add reference data folder to workspace
Always add reference data folder to workspace
Never add reference data folder to workspace
makerchip.addResourcesFolder.description: Add Makerchip reference data folder to workspace (for LLMs)

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_compile
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compile and open TL-Verilog in Makerchip IDE.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens the Makerchip IDE in VS Code and compiles the current or specified TL-Verilog file, showing the compiled circuit diagram, waveforms, and Visual Debug output. IMPORTANT: Use this tool whenever the user wants to SEE, SHOW, DEMONSTRATE, VISUALIZE, or VIEW TL-Verilog code or examples - don't just show code in the chat. Also use when they want to run, compile, open, launch, or test Makerchip or TL-Verilog. When providing TL-Verilog examples, prefer opening them in Makerchip rather than only displaying the code. The visual IDE is the primary way to interact with TL-Verilog.

filePath.description: Optional path to a TL-Verilog file to open and compile. If not provided, uses the currently active editor.

code.description: Optional TL-Verilog code to compile. When provided, creates a new unsaved file with this code and opens it in Makerchip. Use this to show examples or demonstrate TL-Verilog features.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel. Use this to open multiple Makerchip panels for different designs or comparisons.

languageModelTools.name: makerchip_ide_call
languageModelTools.userDescription: Call any Makerchip IDE method directly.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Invokes any method on the Makerchip IDE instance. Available methods include: compile(code), setCode(code, readOnly), getCode(lastChangeGeneration), setCodeFromURL(url, readOnly), activatePane(name), openStaticPane(name, background). Use this for advanced IDE control like changing panes, setting code without compiling, or loading code from URLs. Methods like onReady, onCodeChange, onCompilationLog are callbacks and should not be called directly.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the method. Order matters.

panelName.description: Optional panel name to target. If not provided, uses the default panel.

chatParticipants.name: makerchip
chatParticipants.description: Makerchip IDE integration for TL-Verilog development

scripts.compile: tsc -p ./ && tsc -p src/tsconfig.webview.json && npm run copy-webview
scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
